sources.label: system-block.ts (runtime PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt)
sources.summary: GPT hint is an 8-bullet array joined with newlines; providerHintSection maps structured openai->gpt; unknown/null emits no hint. Design KD1/KD3 mechanics are accurate.

sources.label: gpt.md source markdown
sources.summary: Mirrors PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt today. No existing test enforces runtime<->markdown byte alignment, so design AC2 alignment assertion is a NEW invariant, not a re-check — strengthens correctness.

sources.label: advance-meta spec rq-providerAdvSkinny01.2 / rq-providerAdvMetrics01
sources.summary: Law: structured provider identity emits exactly one matching hint via output.system[0]; no additional system entry; metrics must NOT impose a hard prompt-size cap. Design conforms; adding 3 bullets violates no law.

sources.label: deploy-local.test.ts provider metrics test
sources.summary: provider_hint lines:2 assertion uses an inline fixture ('<!-- PROVIDER_HINT:gpt -->\nHint'), NOT the real gpt.md. Adding bullets to gpt.md does NOT regress this test. AC5 'no size cap' is structurally true.

sources.label: scripts/provider-eval.ts
sources.summary: gpt hint_file is read from disk at eval time; no hardcoded byte/line cap on gpt.md. Confirms AC5 / DONT4.

sources.label: OpenAI GPT-5.5 prompting guide
sources.summary: Authoritative: 'Ask for clarification only when the missing information would materially change the answer or create meaningful risk, and keep any question narrow.' 'Use ALWAYS/NEVER only for true invariants; for judgment calls prefer decision rules.' 'Define success criteria / what must be true.' Design KD2 wording matches this near-verbatim.

sources.label: OpenAI GPT-5 / Codex prompting guides
sources.summary: GPT-5 family is biased toward action and AWAY from clarifying questions ('Bias to action... do not end your turn with clarifications unless truly blocked'). The carve-out is exactly: 'when key information is missing, pause and ask 1-3 brief clarifying questions.' Validates the need for KD2 bullet 3's explicit auto-continue override.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is a narrow, reversible provider-hint DATA change inside the existing single-system-entry architecture. Mechanics (providerHintSection mapping, structured openai->gpt, single output.system[0] append) are described accurately and match source. No spec law is contradicted: advance-meta rq-providerAdvSkinny01.2 permits exactly-one-hint emission and rq-providerAdvMetrics01 explicitly forbids a hard size cap (so adding 3 bullets is in-bounds). The AC2 runtime<->markdown alignment test is a genuinely NEW invariant that closes a real current gap (no such test exists today). External OpenAI GPT-5.5 guidance validates KD2 wording: outcome/success-criteria emphasis, decision-rule (not absolute) clarify trigger, and measurable-acceptance rewrite of subjective terms all match official best practice near-verbatim.

validation.notes: CAUTION-level, non-blocking: (1) Existing GPT bullet 'If user asked to continue/ship, keep going... unless a stop condition above is met' (system-block.ts:133) is a broad auto-continue rule living in the same flat bullet list as the new KD2 clarify directive. KD2 bullet 3 intentionally reconciles them ('no-pause/auto-continue rules do not suppress it'), so this is a designed carve-out, not a contradiction — but a reader must connect two non-adjacent bullets in a now-11-bullet list. Recommend placing the new clarify bullet so the auto-continue override reads cleanly, and confirm ordering in implementation. (2) GPT hint grows 8->11 bullets; OpenAI GPT-5.5 guidance warns against process-heavy prompt stacks and unnecessary absolute rules. The 3 new bullets are decision-rules (good), but verify none use absolute ALWAYS/NEVER framing for judgment calls. (3) AC1 says 'exactly 3 new directives'; ensure the alignment test asserts presence of all 3 substrings AND that non-GPT/null paths exclude them (design already specifies this) so AC1+AC4 are both pinned.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED with minor cautions. Proceed with the design as written. During implementation: (a) order the new clarify bullet near or after the existing continue/ship bullet so the auto-continue override is locally legible; (b) keep all 3 new bullets as decision-rules, avoiding absolute ALWAYS/NEVER for the judgment-call clarify trigger, per OpenAI GPT-5.5 guidance; (c) ensure the new AC2 alignment test normalizes a single trailing newline (design already notes this) since gpt.md ends with a newline and the TS array join does not; (d) keep the pinned non-GPT expectations to satisfy AC3/DONT3 byte-identity.
follow_ups: Consider (separate, optional) collapsing the GPT hint's flat bullet list into a tiny 'when to continue vs ask' decision block if it grows further, per OpenAI guidance against process-heavy stacks — out of scope for this change.

subagent_reports.summary: Inline acceptance review. Diff limited to GPT provider hint source/runtime and regression tests. Contract traceability, correctness/edge cases, security surface, tests/TDD evidence, scope conformance, and architecture quality checked; no blockers or issues found.
follow_ups: Restart OpenCode sessions to pick up deployed host-loaded plugin runtime changes before evaluating live provider-hint behavior.

subagent_reports.summary: Release hardening complete. Merge dry-run clean, smoke verification passed, contract proof passed, review findings have no actionable unresolved blockers/issues. Test coverage, documentation/cleanup, production/deployment, and residual slop dimensions passed. One LOW non-blocking test-hardening suggestion remains: direct GPT-only exclusion test could also include GLM/Kimi provider IDs, but existing byte-pin tests already protect those hints.

items.text: Add GPT-only requirements-rigor guidance that overrides brevity/compression only for requirements text.
Keep runtime provider hint constants and provider hint source markdown aligned.
Preserve Claude, GLM, and Kimi provider hints exactly.
Preserve the single-system-entry provider-hint architecture and metrics reporting.
Build and deploy the plugin update, then surface OpenCode restart requirement.
`PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` contains exactly 3 new requirements-rigor directives.
`.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` contains the same 3 directives as `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt`.
Claude, GLM, and Kimi provider hints remain byte-identical to their pre-change contents.
`assembleSystemBlock({ currentProviderID: "openai" })` includes all 3 new directives; `anthropic`, unknown provider IDs, and `null` do not include them.
Provider metrics still report `provider_hint` bytes/lines, and no prompt-size hard cap is added.
`plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts`, provider metrics tests, `pnpm run build`, and `scripts/deploy-local.sh --fix` pass.
Scope is provider family `gpt` via structured `openai` provider identity; do not target model version only.
Requirements correctness outranks prompt byte minimization, but added directives stay lean and complete-not-verbose.
Existing `advance-meta` laws remain authoritative: exactly one matching hint, no hint for unknown/missing provider identity, provider metrics reporting only.
Live tool behavior in the current OpenCode session cannot be validated after source edits until rebuild/deploy and OpenCode restart because host-loaded plugin modules are cached.
Directive wording must remain aligned with GPT-5.5 best practice: outcome-first, success criteria, validation, narrow material clarification, and no long process-heavy prompt stack.
If tests/build/deploy fail, inspect and remediate in scope, rerun verification, and do not mark acceptance complete until green evidence exists or a real blocker is surfaced.
Do not change the caveman compression overlay.
Do not change shared command files or global ADV runtime behavior for all providers.
Do not modify Claude, GLM, or Kimi provider hints.
Do not introduce a hard prompt-size cap as correctness proof.
Do not recreate generated provider runtime agents or retired provider variants.

documents.proposal:
## Cross-Project Origin

This change was created as a follow-up from **toolbox**.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Source project | toolbox |
| Source path | `/home/jon/toolbox` |

> **Note:** The originating project should be consulted for context on why this change is needed.

## Why

GPT-family ADV sessions under-specify acceptance criteria and skip clarification during idea/problem/proposal/discovery. Root cause: GPT-5.5 reads prompts literally; the global caveman `full` overlay + ADV terse voice contract compress/drop "detail," while AC drafting and Socratic clarification are requirements detail. The AC-quality bar and clarification license sit far from the drafting instruction and are outweighed by emphatic no-pause/auto-continue rules. Desired outcome: GPT-family sessions reliably draft measurable, testable acceptance criteria and run required clarification — with zero behavior change for Claude/GLM/Kimi.

## What Changes

Add 3 directives to GPT-only provider hints:

1. Requirements text (`problem statements`, `clarifying questions`, `acceptance criteria`, `agreements`) is exempt from brevity/voice/compression rules for required details; keep requirements complete, not verbose.
2. Each acceptance criterion must be binary pass/fail, name an observable signal, and be bounded by a number, threshold, or explicit state; reject subjective terms (`fast`, `easy`, `robust`, `clean`) and rewrite before presenting.
3. Clarifying questions during idea/problem/proposal/discovery are required work when missing information would materially change the outcome or create risk; they are not "shall I continue?" prompts, and no-pause/auto-continue rules never suppress them.

Runtime isolation remains provider-family `gpt` through `currentProviderID === "openai"` → `PROVIDER_HINT_BY_PROVIDER_ID.openai = "gpt"`.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` carries the 3 new requirements-rigor directives.
- SC2: `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` carries the same 3 directives so repo hint source and runtime constant stay aligned.
- SC3: `PROVIDER_HINTS.claude`, `.glm`, `.kimi` and `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/{claude,glm,kimi}.md` remain byte-identical to pre-change.
- SC4: For `currentProviderID: "openai"`, assembled system block contains each new directive; for `"anthropic"`, `"unknown"`, and `null`, behavior is unchanged.
- SC5: Provider-hint metrics keep reporting `provider_hint` bytes/lines and no hard prompt-size cap is introduced.
- SC6: `plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts`, provider-hint metrics asset tests, `pnpm run build`, and `scripts/deploy-local.sh --fix` complete clean.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Edit `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` in `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts`.
- Edit `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` to keep provider hint source markdown aligned with runtime constants.
- Add/extend assertions in `plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts` for GPT directives, source/runtime alignment, and non-GPT non-regression.
- Preserve provider-eval metrics behavior in `plugin/src/deploy-local.test.ts` / `scripts/provider-eval.ts`.
- Build + deploy.

### Out of Scope

- Caveman overlay base (`caveman-config.cjs`) — global, affects all models.
- Shared command files (`adv-discover.md`, `adv-proposal.md`, `adv.md`) — affect all models.
- Other provider families (Claude/GLM/Kimi).
- Per-model-version (`gpt-5.5` only) targeting — no structural hook exists; family scope is the boundary.
- The general locality problem (AC bar far from drafting point) in shared files.

### Must Not

- Regress Claude/GLM/Kimi provider-hint bytes or behavior.
- Touch the caveman compression overlay.
- Introduce a hard prompt-size cap as correctness proof (forbidden by advance-meta spec).
- Create generated provider runtime agents or restore retired provider variants.

## Affected Code

- `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts` — `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt`, `PROVIDER_HINT_BY_PROVIDER_ID` behavior remains unchanged.
- `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` — repo source markdown used by provider eval and documented as mirror source.
- `plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts` — provider-hint assertions.
- `plugin/src/deploy-local.test.ts` / `scripts/provider-eval.ts` — should remain behavior-compatible; metrics tests should pass without pinning exact bytes.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Status | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Skill Discovery | PASS | `customize-opencode` loaded for plugin/provider-hint source behavior; `adv-agent-tool-contracts` considered for schema/tool-contract risk; no pending-review skill frontmatter found. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | No `temp/*.md`; `docs/*-prep.md` not relevant to provider-hint prompt behavior. New finding: provider hint source markdown must be updated with runtime constant. |
| Conflict Scan | PASS | Active changes do not overlap; validation passes with expected pre-prep warnings (`NO_TASKS`, `NO_DELTAS`); agenda has unrelated follow-ups only. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases documented below. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | User-facing questions resolved; technical questions resolved from code/spec/docs and online research. |
| Draft Spec Deltas | PASS | No spec deltas required; existing `advance-meta` law covers runtime provider hints and metrics. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Related patterns are provider hint source markdown, runtime constants, system-block tests, deploy/provider-eval metrics, and provider docs. |
| LBP Check | PASS | GPT-only runtime provider hint is the least-blast-radius fix; official OpenAI GPT-5.5 guidance and public user reports support explicit success criteria, bounded AC, and narrow clarification rules. |

### Current State

- Runtime hints live in `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts` and are emitted only from structured provider IDs.
- `openai` maps to `gpt`; unknown/missing provider ID emits no hint.
- `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` is documented as provider-hint source markdown and is read by `scripts/provider-eval.ts`.
- `docs/provider-agent-assembly.md` states runtime constants in `system-block.ts` mirror `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/{provider}.md`.
- `advance-meta` requires exactly one matching provider hint, no hint for unknown/missing identity, and provider-hint bytes/lines metrics without hard cap.

### External Research Validation

- OpenAI `Using GPT-5.5`: GPT-5.5 should be treated as a new model family, not a drop-in replacement; prompts should state expected outcome and success criteria; GPT-5.5 interprets prompts literally and thoroughly; coding agents should be explicit about test expectations, acceptance criteria, and when to continue versus ask for help. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/latest-model
- OpenAI `Prompt guidance`: GPT-5.5 is strongest when prompts define target outcome, success criteria, constraints, and context; avoid unnecessary absolute rules except true invariants; ask for clarification only when missing information materially changes the answer or creates meaningful risk; validation rules and concrete validation commands improve agentic UX. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/prompt-guidance
- OpenAI `Reasoning models`: `reasoning.effort` is a tuning knob, not the primary way to recover quality; agentic/research-heavy workflows need definitions of done and verification. Source: https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/reasoning
- OpenAI Developer Community thread (May 2026): practical consensus favors interactive clarification/natural language upstream, then structured artifacts downstream for consistency, constraints, compatibility, testing, and repeatable output. This matches ADV idea/problem/proposal/discovery clarification followed by strict agreement/AC. Source: https://community.openai.com/t/natural-language-requests-vs-long-structured-prompts-what-actually-improves-performance/1381299
- DEV Community user report (Jun 2026): AI agents stopped building the wrong thing when AC moved from vague wishes to checkable pass/fail statements; advice: replace adjectives with numbers or concrete behaviors and include unhappy paths. Source: https://dev.to/kunalsharda/i-changed-how-i-write-acceptance-criteria-and-my-ai-agent-stopped-building-the-wrong-thing-3j7c
- Secondary summaries (Artem Daniliants, Simi Studio) independently echo official guidance: outcome-first, explicit success criteria/stop rules, verification loops, and careful clarification decision rules. These are corroborative, not primary authority.

Research conclusion: proposed GPT-only provider hint is aligned with best practice. One wording refinement is required: keep the carve-out requirements-only and complete-not-verbose, so it does not contradict OpenAI guidance to avoid long process-heavy prompts.

### Edge Cases

1. Source/runtime drift: updating only `system-block.ts` leaves provider eval and docs source stale. Fix: update `gpt.md` and add alignment assertion.
2. Cross-family bleed: directive accidentally appears in Claude/GLM/Kimi hint. Fix: assert other providers do not contain GPT directives and remain unchanged.
3. Over-broad carve-out: GPT may ignore terse voice for all prose. Fix: wording limits carve-out to requirements text and says complete, not verbose.
4. AC directive too subjective: vague terms remain allowed. Fix: wording requires observable signal + bounded value/state and rejects listed subjective terms.
5. Clarification vs auto-continue conflict: GPT treats required clarifying questions as forbidden pause. Fix: wording explicitly classifies material clarification as required work, not "shall I continue?" prompts.
6. Verification failure path: tests/build/deploy may fail. Fix: inspect first failure, remediate in scope, rerun; if deploy is blocked by unavailable external system or permissions, surface exact blocker and do not mark acceptance complete.

### Open Design Questions

- Q1 (user-facing, resolved): Carve-out breadth. Decision: requirements only.
- Q2 (user-facing, resolved): AC strictness. Decision: strict measurable.
- Q3 (user-facing, resolved): Rollout proof. Decision: tests + build + deploy-local --fix; restart note required.
- Q4 (technical, agent-resolved): Update runtime constant only or source markdown too? Decision: both, because docs/provider-eval treat source markdown as provider-hint source.
- Q5 (technical, agent-resolved): Spec delta needed? Decision: no; existing `rq-providerAdvSkinny01` and `rq-providerAdvMetrics01` already govern this behavior.
- Q6 (user-requested online validation, resolved): Is this best practice for GPT-5.5 and aligned with public user reports? Decision: yes, with requirements-only/complete-not-verbose wording refinement.

### Draft Spec Deltas

No spec deltas required. Existing laws apply:

- `rq-providerAdvSkinny01.2`: known provider emits exactly one matching hint; unknown/missing emits none; one system entry; no heuristic guessing.
- `rq-providerAdvMetrics01.1`: provider eval reports `provider_hint` bytes/lines and does not impose a hard prompt-size cap.

### Related Pattern Scan

Related patterns found:

- `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts` — runtime provider hint constants and structured provider mapping.
- `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/{provider}.md` — source markdown for provider hints.
- `plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts` — emission/unknown/missing/duplicate tests.
- `plugin/src/deploy-local.test.ts` — provider hint asset existence and metrics behavior tests.
- `scripts/provider-eval.ts` — reads provider hint markdown and reports `provider_hint` metrics.
- `docs/provider-agent-assembly.md` + `docs/specs/advance-meta.md` — architecture/spec law.

### LBP Check

Best direction: GPT-only provider hint. It preserves single ADV runtime agent architecture, uses structured provider identity, avoids global caveman/shared command changes, and keeps provider-family isolation. Official OpenAI GPT-5.5 guidance says GPT-5.5 interprets prompts literally and benefits from explicit outcome, success criteria, acceptance criteria, stop rules, and verification guidance. Public user reports match the same pattern: interactive clarification upstream, structured/checkable criteria downstream. Therefore prompt conflict resolution via a narrow provider hint is better than changing reasoning effort or weakening global terse voice.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

Scout skipped — narrow provider-hint data change, no product tradeoff, no viable external implementation alternative after code/spec/online inspection.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

`### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS — no ambiguity findings. Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C`

## Recommended Objectives

1. Add GPT-only requirements-rigor directives that override compression only for requirements text.
2. Keep runtime hint and source markdown aligned.
3. Prove OpenAI/GPT receives the directives while non-GPT providers do not.
4. Preserve provider-hint metrics and single-system-entry architecture.
5. Build, deploy, and surface restart requirement.

## Constraints

- Isolation granularity is provider family `gpt` (`openai`), not model version.
- Keep added lines lean, but requirements correctness outranks prompt byte minimization.
- Stay within `advance-meta`: exactly one matching hint, none for unknown/missing, metrics reporting only, no hard cap.
- If verification fails, inspect, remediate in scope, and rerun; do not call failure a flake or mark acceptance complete without green evidence.

## Impact

Low blast radius: one provider-specific hint constant, matching source markdown, and tests. No spec delta. Requires build + deploy; current OpenCode session will not see live tool behavior until restart because host-loaded plugin modules are cached.
documents.problemStatement: GPT-family sessions running the ADV agent under-specify acceptance criteria and skip clarifying questions during idea/problem/proposal/discovery. Investigated root cause: GPT-5.5 interprets prompts literally (per OpenAI GPT-5.5 guidance); the global caveman `full` compression overlay plus ADV's terse voice contract compress/drop requirements detail, while the AC-quality bar and clarification license sit far from the drafting point and are drowned by emphatic anti-pause rules. Desired outcome: GPT-family sessions reliably draft measurable, testable acceptance criteria and run required clarification — with zero behavior change for Claude/GLM/Kimi.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement

## Objectives

- SC1: Add GPT-only requirements-rigor guidance that overrides brevity/compression only for requirements text.
- SC2: Keep runtime provider hint constants and provider hint source markdown aligned.
- SC3: Preserve Claude, GLM, and Kimi provider hints exactly.
- SC4: Preserve the single-system-entry provider-hint architecture and metrics reporting.
- SC5: Build and deploy the plugin update, then surface OpenCode restart requirement.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` contains exactly 3 new requirements-rigor directives.
- AC2: `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` contains the same 3 directives as `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt`.
- AC3: Claude, GLM, and Kimi provider hints remain byte-identical to their pre-change contents.
- AC4: `assembleSystemBlock({ currentProviderID: "openai" })` includes all 3 new directives; `anthropic`, unknown provider IDs, and `null` do not include them.
- AC5: Provider metrics still report `provider_hint` bytes/lines, and no prompt-size hard cap is added.
- AC6: `plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts`, provider metrics tests, `pnpm run build`, and `scripts/deploy-local.sh --fix` pass.

## Constraints

- C1: Scope is provider family `gpt` via structured `openai` provider identity; do not target model version only.
- C2: Requirements correctness outranks prompt byte minimization, but added directives stay lean and complete-not-verbose.
- C3: Existing `advance-meta` laws remain authoritative: exactly one matching hint, no hint for unknown/missing provider identity, provider metrics reporting only.
- C4: Live tool behavior in the current OpenCode session cannot be validated after source edits until rebuild/deploy and OpenCode restart because host-loaded plugin modules are cached.
- C5: Directive wording must remain aligned with GPT-5.5 best practice: outcome-first, success criteria, validation, narrow material clarification, and no long process-heavy prompt stack.
- C6: If tests/build/deploy fail, inspect and remediate in scope, rerun verification, and do not mark acceptance complete until green evidence exists or a real blocker is surfaced.

## Avoidances

- DONT1: Do not change the caveman compression overlay.
- DONT2: Do not change shared command files or global ADV runtime behavior for all providers.
- DONT3: Do not modify Claude, GLM, or Kimi provider hints.
- DONT4: Do not introduce a hard prompt-size cap as correctness proof.
- DONT5: Do not recreate generated provider runtime agents or retired provider variants.

## Preview Applicability

visual_surface: false

Rationale: This change affects runtime prompt/provider-hint text and tests only. It has no browser-visible UI, visual output, or frontend rendering surface.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

- Carve-out breadth: requirements only. Problem statements, clarifying questions, acceptance criteria, and agreements are exempt from brevity/compression for required details; normal terse voice remains elsewhere.
- AC strictness: strict measurable. Acceptance criteria must be binary pass/fail, observable, and bounded; subjective terms are rejected and rewritten.
- Rollout proof: build + deploy. Acceptance expects tests/build plus `scripts/deploy-local.sh --fix`, followed by OpenCode restart note.
- Acceptance criteria approved at discovery checkpoint by user reply: `approve`.
- User-requested online validation: research online and confirm best practice / public user success before implementation.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

- Update both `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts` and `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` because docs/provider-eval treat source markdown as provider-hint source and runtime constants mirror it.
- No spec delta required because `rq-providerAdvSkinny01.2` and `rq-providerAdvMetrics01.1` already govern the affected behavior.
- GPT-only provider hint is the least-blast-radius fix: it resolves literal prompt conflict for GPT without global caveman/shared-command changes.
- Official OpenAI GPT-5.5 docs support the direction: define outcomes/success criteria/acceptance criteria, clarify only when missing information materially changes outcome or risk, validate outputs, and do not rely on higher reasoning effort to fix conflicting instructions.
- Public user reports support the same shape: natural conversation/clarification upstream, structured/checkable artifacts downstream, and pass/fail AC with numbers/concrete behavior instead of adjectives.

## Deferred Questions

None.

## Sign-Off

Acceptance criteria approved by user reply `approve`. Online research requested by user was completed and incorporated without changing approved AC.
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview

This is a provider-hint data change inside the existing single ADV runtime agent architecture.

Runtime path:

1. OpenCode/plugin system transform receives `currentProviderID`.
2. `providerHintSection()` maps structured provider ID through `PROVIDER_HINT_BY_PROVIDER_ID`.
3. `openai` maps to `gpt`; unknown or missing provider identity returns no provider hint.
4. The selected hint string is appended through the existing `output.system[0]` system-block path.

Change shape:

- Update only the GPT provider hint payload in `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts`.
- Update the mirrored GPT source markdown in `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md`.
- Keep Claude/GLM/Kimi hint payloads and source files unchanged.
- Extend tests to prove OpenAI/GPT receives the new requirements-rigor guidance and non-GPT providers do not.

## Key Decisions

### KD1 — Provider-hint injection, not global ADV/caveman changes

Use the existing GPT-only provider hint because the failure is GPT-family literal prompt conflict. This avoids changing all providers, shared command files, or the global caveman compression overlay.

Rationale:

- OpenAI GPT-5.5 docs recommend outcome-first prompts with explicit success criteria, constraints, stopping/clarification rules, and validation.
- The current failure is not lack of model intelligence; it is conflicting instructions around terse voice vs requirements detail.
- `PROVIDER_HINT_BY_PROVIDER_ID.openai = "gpt"` gives the required family-level isolation.

### KD2 — Requirements-only, complete-not-verbose carve-out

Directive wording must not grant broad permission to ignore terse voice. It should say requirements artifacts are exempt from compression only when required details would otherwise be dropped.

Final design wording:

1. `Requirements artifacts (problem statements, clarifying questions, acceptance criteria, agreements) are exempt from brevity/compression when detail is required; keep them complete, specific, and testable, not verbose.`
2. `Acceptance criteria must be pass/fail, name an observable signal, and be bounded by a number, threshold, or explicit state. Rewrite subjective terms like fast/easy/robust/clean before presenting.`
3. `During idea/problem/proposal/discovery, ask narrow clarifying questions when missing information would materially change outcome, acceptance boundary, or risk. This is required work, not "shall I continue?", so no-pause/auto-continue rules do not suppress it.`

Rationale:

- Matches OpenAI guidance: clarify only when missing information materially changes outcome or risk.
- Preserves caveman/terse voice for non-requirements prose.
- Makes AC quality structural enough for tests/review: pass/fail, observable, bounded.

### KD3 — Runtime constant and source markdown stay aligned

Update both:

- `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts` → runtime `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt`
- `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` → source markdown used by provider eval/docs

Rationale:

- `docs/provider-agent-assembly.md` says runtime constants mirror provider markdown.
- `scripts/provider-eval.ts` reads `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` for provider hint metrics/evaluation.
- Updating only runtime would pass live behavior but leave evaluation/documentation stale.

### KD4 — No ADR

No ADR draft. The change is reversible, narrow, and already governed by `advance-meta` provider-hint architecture requirements.

## Implementation Strategy

1. Add the 3 GPT-only directive strings after the existing GPT completion-discipline lines in `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt`.
2. Add identical 3 bullets to `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md`.
3. Extend `plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts`:
   - import `PROVIDER_HINTS` if needed;
   - assert `openai` assembled system block contains all 3 directive substrings;
   - assert `anthropic`, unknown provider, and `null` do not contain the new directive substrings;
   - assert `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` matches `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` exactly after normalizing one optional trailing newline;
   - pin Claude/GLM/Kimi provider hint strings or source files to their existing exact values so accidental edits fail fast.
4. Leave `scripts/provider-eval.ts` behavior unchanged. Existing deploy/provider-eval asset tests prove `provider_hint` metrics remain present and no hard size cap exists.
5. Verification order:
   - targeted: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/utils/system-block.test.ts src/deploy-local.test.ts`
   - build: `pnpm run build` from `plugin/`
   - deploy: `scripts/deploy-local.sh --fix` from repo root
6. If any verification fails, inspect first failure, remediate in scope, and rerun. Do not call failures flaky without rerun/log proof.

## LBP Analysis

This design follows current GPT-5.5 prompting best practice:

- define outcomes and success criteria rather than adding process-heavy global prompt rules;
- clarify only when missing context materially changes outcome/risk;
- keep directives concise but explicit where product contract requires it;
- validate with executable tests and build/deploy evidence;
- keep provider-specific behavior behind structured provider identity rather than heuristic model-name guessing.

It also matches public practitioner reports: use conversation/clarification upstream to discover the right requirement, then structured checkable acceptance criteria downstream for repeatability and testability.

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts`
  - `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` only.
  - `PROVIDER_HINT_BY_PROVIDER_ID` unchanged.
  - `providerHintSection()` unchanged.
- `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md`
  - source markdown mirror for GPT provider hint.
- `plugin/src/utils/system-block.test.ts`
  - provider-hint assertions and source/runtime alignment test.
- Existing tests to keep green:
  - `plugin/src/deploy-local.test.ts` provider hint assets / metrics.
  - Any system-block ordering/unknown-provider tests.

## Contract Coverage Plan

- AC1 / AC4: `system-block.test.ts` asserts GPT runtime string contains directives and non-GPT/missing provider paths do not.
- AC2: source/runtime alignment assertion compares `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` to `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md`.
- AC3: pinned non-GPT expectations and/or git diff review prove Claude/GLM/Kimi unchanged.
- AC5: existing provider metrics tests remain green; no prompt-size cap introduced.
- AC6: targeted tests, build, and deploy command outputs provide evidence.
- C4 / SC5: final acceptance notes must remind user to restart OpenCode after deploy because host-loaded plugin modules are cached.

## Risks / Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| GPT hint becomes too verbose and conflicts with OpenAI best practice | Use exactly 3 short bullets; requirements-only carve-out; complete-not-verbose wording. |
| Non-GPT behavior changes accidentally | Do not edit non-GPT source files; add test/pinned expectations; review git diff. |
| Runtime/source drift | Alignment test between `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` and `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md`. |
| Metrics regress due hint length | Keep provider-eval behavior unchanged; run provider metrics asset tests. |
| Live session does not reflect source change | Build + deploy, then tell user to restart OpenCode. |
| Deploy/test failure | Inspect, remediate, rerun; surface true blockers only. |

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout: skipped — trivially scoped provider-hint data/test change. Opportunity surface is limited to source/runtime alignment and test hardening, both already adopted in design. No user-value tradeoff or broader architecture alternative remains after discovery research.

## Validator Result

Verdict: CAUTION — proceed.

Independent validator assessment:

- Mechanics accurate: structured `openai` → `gpt`, unknown/null provider emits no hint, single-system-entry path preserved.
- Source/runtime alignment test is a real new guard; no existing test enforces `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` equals `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md`.
- No spec-law contradiction: `rq-providerAdvSkinny01.2` and `rq-providerAdvMetrics01.1` allow this and forbid hard size caps.
- Provider metrics behavior remains structurally safe because provider-eval reads `gpt.md` without byte/line caps.
- KD2 wording matches OpenAI GPT-5.5 prompt guidance.

Non-blocking cautions adopted for implementation:

1. Keep the clarify directive as a decision-rule; avoid absolute ALWAYS/NEVER wording for the judgment-call trigger.
2. Order the clarify/auto-continue override near the existing GPT auto-continue bullet so precedence reads cleanly.
3. Pin AC1 and AC4 in the same provider-hint test, and normalize one optional trailing newline for source/runtime comparison.

Recommendation: proceed as written.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome

Added GPT-only requirements-rigor guidance so GPT sessions keep requirements artifacts complete, specific, and testable despite terse voice/compression rules. Acceptance review is approved with no blockers or issues; the change is non-visual and requires restarting OpenCode sessions after deploy before live host-loaded runtime behavior changes.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Added three requirements-rigor directives to `PROVIDER_HINTS.gpt` in `plugin/src/utils/system-block.ts`.
2. Added the same three directives to `.opencode/agent-parts/providers/gpt.md` so runtime constants and provider hint source markdown stay aligned.
3. Added regression coverage for GPT-only directive inclusion, source/runtime alignment, non-GPT provider byte identity, and provider metrics/no hard-cap behavior.
4. Verified build/deploy path and preserved the OpenCode restart notice for cached host-loaded plugin modules.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: APPROVED with 0 blockers, 0 issues, 0 suggestions, 0 nits.
- Tests: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/utils/system-block.test.ts src/deploy-local.test.ts` passed 128/128 during review; task evidence also records `pnpm run build`, `scripts/deploy-local.sh --fix`, smoke, and full suite passed after rerun.
- Static checks: `git diff --check trunk...HEAD` passed; `adv_change_validate strict:true` passed with only expected `NO_DELTAS` warning.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — agreement declares `visual_surface: false`, and implementation touches provider-hint source/runtime/tests only, with no browser-visible output.
- Contract matrix: 22 required rows passed/respected; 0 failed, violated, unknown, or missing evidence rows.

## Remaining Concerns

OpenCode sessions must restart to pick up deployed host-loaded plugin runtime changes.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
Proposal references authentication/authorization without specifying the model. What auth mechanism will be used?
